steps.title: Identify condition for x-intercepts
steps.content:
The x-intercepts occur where the graph crosses the x-axis, i.e., where $y = f(x) = 0$.
Set the function equal to zero:
$$f(x)=4(x-2)(x-7)(x-11)=0$$
Since the constant factor $4$ is never zero, the product $(x-2)(x-7)(x-11)$ must be zero.

steps.title: Apply the zero-product property
steps.content:
If a product of factors equals zero, at least one factor is zero.
Thus we set each linear factor equal to zero:
$$x-2=0$$
$$x-7=0$$
$$x-11=0$$
Solving each gives the possible x-coordinates.

steps.title: List the intercepts and select the matching choice
steps.content:
The solutions are $x=2$, $x=7$, and $x=11$. Each of these is an x-coordinate of an x-intercept.
Since this is a free-response question, any of these three values is an acceptable answer. The answer is 2, 7, or 11.
